items.user_turn: Please convert my drafted seven-day Kyoto itinerary into a reservation-ready, time-stamped plan. Travel: arrive Kansai Intl (KIX) at 09:35 on April 10, 2026 (JL123) and depart Kansai Intl at 14:20 on April 17, 2026 (JL456). Accommodation: Sakura Guesthouse (Kyoto); check-in 15:00, check-out 11:00. Mobility: I have mild knee osteoarthritis, use a cane occasionally, prefer walking segments of 500 meters or less without a bench, need a rest about every 20 minutes, and require wheelchair-accessible entrances when available. Budget: 120,000 JPY per person for sightseeing and meals; I prefer to avoid high-end kaiseki unless essential. I'm traveling with my partner, Alex Kim. Please include precise transit directions and durations between stops with station names, suggested meal time windows and restaurant/temple reservation suggestions, luggage-storage options, accessibility adjustments for my mobility needs, and a rough per-day cost estimate so I can book tickets and restaurants.

items.user_turn: Please turn the solo-safety protocol into a concrete, shareable plan for my upcoming weekend trip: I need a timed check-in schedule with escalation windows, ready-to-send emergency message templates (SMS and email), a guesthouse verification and arrival checklist, and step-by-step actions if I miss a check-in. Trip itinerary and times: depart Hometown by car at 8:00 AM on March 27, 2026 (Hometown -> Seaside, ~2.5 hours), expected arrival about 10:30 AM; expected late guesthouse arrival around 10:00 PM on March 27; return depart Seaside at 6:00 PM on March 29, 2026. Guesthouse details: Blue Fern Guesthouse, 78 Harbor Rd, Seaside, State, 54321; Reservation #BF-4592; front desk +1-555-222-3344; expected check-in ~10:00 PM on March 27. Emergency contact: Alex Morgan, friend, 555-123-4567. Medical info for emergencies: diagnosed generalized anxiety disorder, escitalopram 10 mg daily, allergic to penicillin. For check-ins I prefer an initial departure check, an ETA check, an evening arrival check, and location updates roughly every four hours while traveling. Can you build the timed schedule, pre-filled messages, guesthouse arrival checklist, and a clear escalation plan if I miss a check-in?

items.user_turn: Please turn my master packing checklist and five-item toiletry consolidation into a finalized, city-by-city lightweight packing plan (carry-on-first). My flights: AA1243 JFK→ORD (morning), carry-on only, 7 kg limit; AA457 ORD→DEN (midday), allows one checked bag up to 23 kg; BA302 ORD→LHR (evening), allows one checked bag up to 23 kg plus one carry-on up to 8 kg. Accommodation contacts: Midtown apartment — 123 University Lane, Hometown, State, 12345 (self-rented), no on-site laundry; Lisbon guesthouse — Rua das Flores 22, 1100-123 Lisbon, Portugal; contact Rosa Martinez, 555-123-4567; has coin laundry. I have no special event wardrobe needs. Medical needs that affect packing: levothyroxine 100 mcg (must be carry-on), severe peanut allergy (EpiPen), and daily soft contact lenses with solution. I want prioritized outfit rotation, a laundry schedule keyed to the Lisbon coin laundry, exact allowed bag sizes/weights per flight accounted for, recommended refillable toiletry container sizes, and a pared-down packing list for each stop — starting with carry-on-first.

items.user_turn: I need help finalizing a detailed, accessible weekend itinerary for our three-generation family: day-by-day schedules with morning activity blocks and relaxed afternoons; time-sensitive reservations and accessibility requests for tickets, restaurants, and transport; room and transport seat assignments; and a printable itinerary with a packing and medication checklist. We'll arrive the afternoon of May 14, 2026 at 3:30 PM and depart the morning of May 16, 2026 at 11:00 AM. Health details to plan around: Helen Parker (78) uses a walker, has limited stair tolerance, and takes antihypertensive medication morning and evening; George Parker (76) has limited stamina and a history of angina, prefers short walks and frequent rests, and carries nitroglycerin; Lucas Parker (3) has a severe peanut allergy and carries an EpiPen. Budget is $2,000 total for the weekend. Lodging/contact details: emily.parker@goggle.com, 555-123-4567, 123 University Lane, Hometown, State, 12345. Can you finalize reservations, make the accessibility requests, assign rooms/seats as needed, and produce a printable itinerary and medication/packing checklist tailored to these constraints?
